_comment: Machine-readable quality benchmark for The LLM Stack. Source: https://cs336.stanford.edu/ (Stanford CS336, Language Modeling from Scratch). Each unit is one audit item. Grading rubric lives in scripts/gen_audit_workflow.py.

rubric.0: ABSENT — topic not present in the book
rubric.1: MENTIONED — named or gestured at, no working understanding conveyed
rubric.2: EXPLAINED — concept correctly explained, but a reader could NOT implement it from the text alone
rubric.3: BUILDABLE — a competent reader could implement a correct version from the book alone (complete algorithm, shapes, edge cases, hyperparameters)
rubric.4: REFERENCE — buildable AND includes verification (tests/expected values), failure modes, resource accounting, and links to real library implementations

target: Every unit at >= 3; all assignment units and core-path lectures at 4.

units.title: Overview, tokenization
objectives: Why tokenization exists; bytes vs characters vs subwords
BPE training algorithm end-to-end (pair counting, merge table construction)
BPE encoding/decoding with the merge table; byte-level BPE and the GPT-2 byte encoder
WordPiece and Unigram/SentencePiece contrasts
Pathologies: numbers, whitespace, code, multilingual, vocabulary size tradeoffs
units.buildable_deliverable: Train a byte-level BPE tokenizer from a raw text corpus and round-trip encode/decode correctly.

units.title: PyTorch (einops), resource accounting (FLOPs, memory, arithmetic intensity)
objectives: einops/einsum notation for tensor manipulation
Counting parameters of a Transformer exactly
Counting training and inference FLOPs (the 6ND rule and where it comes from)
Memory accounting: params + grads + optimizer state + activations
Arithmetic intensity and the compute-vs-memory-bound distinction
torch tensor internals: strides, views, contiguity, dtype
units.buildable_deliverable: Given a model config and hardware, compute a correct FLOP and memory budget and predict step time.

units.title: Architectures, hyperparameters
objectives: Pre-norm vs post-norm, RMSNorm, residual stream
Activation functions and the SwiGLU family; FFN sizing
RoPE and positional scheme choices
Width/depth/head-count ratios and standard hyperparameter recipes
Initialization schemes and their justification
units.buildable_deliverable: Specify and justify every hyperparameter of a modern decoder-only LM.

units.title: Attention alternatives and mixture of experts
objectives: MQA/GQA/MLA and their KV-cache implications
Linear attention, SSMs, Mamba, sliding-window and sparse attention
MoE routing (top-k, expert choice), load balancing losses, capacity factor
MoE systems concerns: expert parallelism, all-to-all
units.buildable_deliverable: Implement a top-k routed MoE FFN layer with an auxiliary load-balancing loss.

units.title: GPUs, TPUs
objectives: SM/warp/thread hierarchy, memory hierarchy (HBM/L2/SMEM/registers)
Tensor cores and supported dtypes
TPU systolic arrays and pods; contrast with GPUs
Interconnect: NVLink, PCIe, InfiniBand and their bandwidths
Roofline reasoning applied to real kernels
units.buildable_deliverable: Predict whether a given layer is compute- or memory-bound and explain the hardware reason.

units.title: Kernels, Triton
objectives: The Triton programming model: programs, blocks, masks, pointers
Writing and benchmarking a fused kernel
Tiling, occupancy, autotuning
The online softmax and FlashAttention derivation
Numerical correctness testing against a reference
units.buildable_deliverable: Write a working Triton FlashAttention-2 forward and backward kernel.

units.title: Parallelism I
objectives: Collectives: all-reduce, reduce-scatter, all-gather, all-to-all and their costs
Data parallelism, gradient synchronization, bucketing/overlap
ZeRO stages 1/2/3 and FSDP sharding semantics
Activation checkpointing interaction
units.buildable_deliverable: Implement distributed data-parallel training with overlapped gradient communication.

units.title: Parallelism II
objectives: Tensor parallelism: row/column partitioning and the required collectives
Pipeline parallelism: GPipe, 1F1B, interleaved schedules, bubble analysis
Sequence/context parallelism and ring attention
Composing 3D/4D parallelism; choosing a parallelism plan for a cluster
units.buildable_deliverable: Choose and justify a full parallelism plan for a given model size and cluster topology.

units.title: Scaling laws I
objectives: Power-law form; Kaplan vs Chinchilla
Compute-optimal allocation of parameters vs tokens
IsoFLOP profiles and the parametric loss fit
units.buildable_deliverable: Derive the compute-optimal N/D split for a fixed FLOP budget.

units.title: Scaling laws II (fitting and practice)
objectives: Empirically FITTING a scaling law from a sweep of small runs
Extrapolation risk, fit diagnostics, and confidence
Hyperparameter transfer (muP) across scales
Using scaling laws to make real training decisions
units.buildable_deliverable: Run a small-model sweep, fit a scaling law, and project the loss of a larger run.

units.title: Inference
objectives: Prefill vs decode; why decode is memory-bound
KV cache math and PagedAttention
Continuous batching and scheduling
Speculative decoding
Quantization for serving; latency/throughput tradeoffs and metrics
units.buildable_deliverable: Implement KV-cached autoregressive generation and reason about its throughput limits.

units.title: Evaluation
objectives: Perplexity and its limits
Benchmark families; few-shot prompting protocol sensitivity
Contamination detection
LLM-as-judge, human eval, and arena-style ranking
Statistical significance and error bars in eval
units.buildable_deliverable: Build a correct, contamination-aware evaluation harness for a model.

units.title: Data (sources, datasets)
objectives: Common Crawl: WARC/WET formats and the extraction pipeline
Major open corpora (C4, The Pile, RefinedWeb, Dolma, FineWeb) and how each was built
Licensing, copyright, PII considerations
Tokenizing and sharding a corpus for training
units.buildable_deliverable: Turn a raw Common Crawl dump into clean extracted text.

units.title: Data (filtering, deduplication, mixing, synthetic data)
objectives: Quality filtering: heuristics and classifier-based
Exact and fuzzy dedup: MinHash/LSH, suffix arrays, Bloom filters
Data mixing and domain weighting (DoReMi-style)
Synthetic data generation and its risks
Curriculum and annealing / mid-training data
units.buildable_deliverable: Implement MinHash-LSH near-duplicate removal and a quality classifier over a corpus.

units.title: Mid/post-training (SFT/RLHF)
objectives: SFT data construction, chat templates, loss masking on prompts
Reward modeling from preference pairs (Bradley-Terry)
The full PPO-for-LM loop: rollouts, advantages, KL penalty, value head
DPO and its derivation from the RLHF objective
units.buildable_deliverable: Implement SFT with correct loss masking, and a DPO training step.

units.title: Post-training — RLVR
objectives: Verifiable rewards for math/code; grading and answer extraction
GRPO and critic-free estimators; group advantage normalization
The generation-training loop and rollout engines
Reward hacking and length bias in RLVR
units.buildable_deliverable: Implement GRPO for math reasoning with a verifiable reward function.

units.title: Alignment — multimodality
objectives: Safety alignment objectives and refusal training
Vision-language architectures: encoders, projectors, early vs late fusion
Multimodal data and training stages
Audio/speech and any-to-any models
units.buildable_deliverable: Specify a VLM architecture and its training stages end to end.

units.title: Guest lecture: Daniel Selsam
objectives: Reasoning models, formal math, and frontier research directions

units.title: Guest lecture: Dan Fu
objectives: Efficient architectures, SSMs, and hardware-aware algorithm design

units.title: Assignment 1: Basics
deliverables: Byte-level BPE tokenizer: training and encode/decode
Transformer LM from scratch: embeddings, RoPE, RMSNorm, SwiGLU FFN, causal multi-head attention, weight tying
Cross-entropy loss and AdamW optimizer implemented from scratch
Cosine LR schedule with warmup, gradient clipping
Training loop with checkpointing, and a decoding/sampling function
Train a minimal LM to a target loss

units.title: Assignment 2: Systems
deliverables: Benchmark and profile model layers (timing, nsys/torch profiler, memory)
Triton FlashAttention-2 forward AND backward kernel
Correctness testing of kernels against a PyTorch reference
Distributed data-parallel training implementation with communication overlap
Optimizer state sharding (ZeRO-style) implementation

units.title: Assignment 3: Scaling
deliverables: Reason about each Transformer component's contribution to loss and cost
Design a sweep under a constrained compute budget
Fit IsoFLOP / parametric scaling laws to observed runs
Project optimal model size and token count for a target budget

units.title: Assignment 4: Data
deliverables: Extract text from raw Common Crawl WARC/WET dumps
Language ID, quality filtering, and harmful-content filtering
Exact and fuzzy (MinHash-LSH) deduplication
Measure the downstream effect of filtering choices on model quality

units.title: Assignment 5: Alignment and Reasoning RL
deliverables: Supervised finetuning for math reasoning with correct loss masking
Verifiable reward function and answer parsing for math
GRPO/policy-gradient training loop implementation
Optional: DPO and safety alignment methods

units.title: END-TO-END: train an LLM from scratch
objectives: A single coherent path a reader can follow from raw text to a trained, sampled-from model
Every stage connected: data -> tokenizer -> model -> optimizer -> training loop -> checkpoint -> eval -> inference
Concrete configs and compute budgets at multiple scales (laptop / 1 GPU / 8 GPU / multi-node)
Explicit statement of what to expect: loss curves, timings, failure signatures
units.buildable_deliverable: A reader with GPUs can go from raw corpus to a working trained LM using only this book.

units.title: Verification, testing and debugging discipline
objectives: How to unit-test each component (tokenizer round-trip, attention vs reference, grad-check)
Expected numeric values and sanity checks at each stage
Diagnosing loss spikes, NaNs, divergence, dead experts, silent shape bugs
Profiling workflow to find the actual bottleneck
units.buildable_deliverable: A reader can independently verify each component they build is correct.

units.title: Open-source library coverage across the stack
objectives: For each layer, the real libraries: PyTorch, Triton, HF transformers/datasets/tokenizers, Megatron-LM, DeepSpeed, FSDP, TRL, veRL, vLLM, SGLang, llama.cpp, FlashAttention, xformers, lm-eval-harness
How each library implements the concept just taught, and where to read its source
When to use the library vs write it yourself
units.buildable_deliverable: A reader knows which library to reach for and how it works internally.
